title: Run npm install + npm run build end-to-end clean
description:
## Context
Part of cleanup epic (gviz-3gq). Final gate — everything must build from a cold install after all package removals.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json` then `npm install` succeeds with no errors
- [ ] `npm run build` completes with no warnings or errors
- [ ] Built app loads in a browser and renders correctly
- [ ] No console errors at startup

## Implementation Steps
1. Clean install
2. Build
3. Serve the build output (`npx serve build`) and smoke test

## Files
- package.json
- package-lock.json

## Notes
P0 — this is the release gate.

title: Wire reset.css and typography.css into src/index.js
description:
## Context
The new reset + typography files must be imported globally for the tokens system to take visual effect. Foundation layer keeps MUI CssBaseline untouched (additive only).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/index.js imports "./design/reset.css"
- [ ] src/index.js imports "./design/typography.css"
- [ ] Both imports appear near the top of the file, before App import
- [ ] MUI CssBaseline remains in place (not removed)
- [ ] App still renders; no console errors; build succeeds

## Implementation Steps
1. Open src/index.js
2. Add `import "./design/reset.css";` and `import "./design/typography.css";` above the App import (after React/ReactDOM imports)
3. Do NOT remove any existing CssBaseline, typeface-roboto, or theme-provider code
4. Run `npm start` locally and confirm page still loads; run `npm run build` and confirm success

## Files
- src/index.js

## Notes
Depends on tokens/reset/typography files already landing. Ordering matters only in that our reset/typography come after MUI baseline so they take precedence where they overlap.

title: Inventory @mui/icons-material usages in src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md
description:
## Context
Before we can replace MUI icons with custom SVGs we need an exhaustive list of every icon currently imported. Each icon will later get its own child task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/components/icons/ directory exists
- [ ] src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md exists
- [ ] INVENTORY.md lists every unique @mui/icons-material icon used anywhere in src/
- [ ] For each icon: name, number of import sites, one example file path
- [ ] No icon SVGs are implemented yet (that is child-task work)

## Implementation Steps
1. Create directory src/components/icons/
2. Run: `grep -rhE "from \"@mui/icons-material" src/ | sort -u` and related `grep -rnE "@mui/icons-material/[A-Za-z]+" src/`
3. Parse the output to extract unique icon names
4. For each icon record count + one example path
5. Write results to src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md as a markdown table
6. Add a header section listing total unique icons and total import sites

## Files
- src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md

## Notes
Inventory-only. Do NOT create .jsx icon components yet.

title: Create src/components/ui/index.js barrel file
description:
## Context
Primitives (Button, Dialog, Menu, etc.) will land in src/components/ui/ over several child tasks. A barrel file lets callers import from a stable path from day one.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/components/ui/index.js exists
- [ ] File compiles (empty barrel is fine)
- [ ] Contains a header comment describing its role

## Implementation Steps
1. Create src/components/ui/ directory (if missing)
2. Create src/components/ui/index.js containing a header comment and no exports yet (or `export {};`)

## Files
- src/components/ui/index.js

## Notes
Future primitives will `export { Button } from "./Button";` from this file.

title: Create src/design/typography.css with fontsource imports + utility classes
description:
## Context
Fontsource requires explicit imports per weight. We also want small utility classes (.t-h1 .. .t-mono) so non-primitive code can opt in to the new type system without inline styles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/typography.css imports @fontsource/space-grotesk/400.css, 600.css, 800.css
- [ ] Imports @fontsource/jetbrains-mono/400.css and 600.css
- [ ] Defines utility classes: .t-h1, .t-h2, .t-h3, .t-h4, .t-body, .t-caption, .t-mono, .t-label
- [ ] Sizes match TYPOGRAPHY.sizes in tokens.js (h1=32, h2=24, h3=20, h4=16, body=14, caption=12)
- [ ] .t-mono uses JetBrains Mono stack; everything else uses Space Grotesk stack
- [ ] .t-label uppercase, letter-spacing: 0.05em, font-weight: 600, size 12

## Implementation Steps
1. Create src/design/typography.css
2. Add @import statements for each fontsource weight file
3. Add the utility class selectors with matching font-size / font-weight / line-height
4. Keep line-heights tight (1.1 for h1/h2, 1.2 for h3/h4, 1.4 body)

## Files
- src/design/typography.css

## Notes
Will be imported from src/index.js in a later task.

title: Create src/design/reset.css minimal CSS reset
description:
## Context
We need a minimal CSS reset that sets our baseline (black on white, border-box, Space Grotesk body) without nuking everything MUI CssBaseline does. Foundation layer only adds, does not subtract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/reset.css exists
- [ ] `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }` applied
- [ ] body and all headings have margin: 0; padding: 0
- [ ] body: font-family uses Space Grotesk stack with system-ui fallback, color:#000, background:#fff
- [ ] kbd, code, pre, samp: font-family uses JetBrains Mono stack

## Implementation Steps
1. Create src/design/reset.css
2. Write selectors above with concrete values
3. Do not add normalization that conflicts with MUI CssBaseline (leave list-style, buttons, etc. alone for now)

## Files
- src/design/reset.css

## Notes
Pure CSS file. Will be imported from src/index.js in a later task.

title: Create src/design/tokens.js with design token exports
description:
## Context
Need JS-accessible design tokens plus a CSS-variables injection string. Primitives import from tokens.js; the CSS var string is applied once globally.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/tokens.js exports named consts: COLORS, SPACING, TYPOGRAPHY, BORDERS, SHADOWS, MOTION
- [ ] Values match STYLE.md
- [ ] Also exports a CSS variables string (e.g. `export const cssVariables = ":root { --color-fg: #000; ... }"`) ready to inject via a <style> tag or appended to index.css

## Implementation Steps
1. Create src/design/tokens.js
2. Define:
   - COLORS = { fg:"#000", bg:"#fff", accent:"#4ed1f8", error:"#ff3030", muted:"#666" }
   - SPACING = { xs:4, sm:8, md:16, lg:24, xl:32 }
   - TYPOGRAPHY = { ui:"\"Space Grotesk\", system-ui, sans-serif", mono:"\"JetBrains Mono\", ui-monospace, monospace", sizes:{h1:32,h2:24,h3:20,h4:16,body:14,caption:12} }
   - BORDERS = { thin:"1px solid #000", thick:"2px solid #000", radius:0 }
   - SHADOWS = { hover:"2px 2px 0 0 #000" }
   - MOTION = { duration:"80ms", easing:"linear" }
3. Build cssVariables string from the above
4. Re-export everything as a default object too for convenience

## Files
- src/design/tokens.js

## Notes
Keep flat + serializable. No functions inside the objects.

title: Create src/design/ directory and STYLE.md brutalist style guide
description:
## Context
Need a single source of truth for the new brutalist/minimalist visual language. Everyone implementing primitives should be able to open STYLE.md and know exactly what 1px border color, spacing unit, hover shadow offset, etc. to use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/ directory exists
- [ ] src/design/STYLE.md exists and documents every rule below
- [ ] Doc includes: border-radius = 0; borders = 1px or 2px solid #000; palette = black/white monochrome + #4ed1f8 accent + #ff3030 error; spacing scale 4/8/16/24/32; hover shadow = hard 2px-offset, no blur; no gradients anywhere; transitions = linear 80ms; focus ring = 2px solid black; type stack = Space Grotesk (UI) / JetBrains Mono (code)

## Implementation Steps
1. Create directory src/design/
2. Write src/design/STYLE.md with sections: Principles, Color, Typography, Spacing, Borders, Shadows, Motion, Focus
3. For each section list concrete values and a short "do / do not" bullet

## Files
- src/design/STYLE.md

## Notes
Doc-only; no code impact yet. This is the reference for tokens.js and every primitive.

4. Commit package.json + lockfile

- [ ] src/design/STYLE.md documents all rules

## Implementation Steps
See child issues.

## Notes
This epic must complete before E2 primitives can be built.

title: Keyboard navigation pass — every dialog/menu
description:
## Context
Part of QA & visual regression (gviz-rdr). Manual keyboard-only walkthrough to confirm focus management parity with MUI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every dialog opens via its trigger's Enter/Space
- [ ] Every dialog closes via ESC
- [ ] Tab order inside each dialog is sensible (primary action reachable without excessive tabs)
- [ ] Focus is trapped while a dialog is open
- [ ] On close, focus returns to the element that opened the dialog
- [ ] Every menu navigates with Arrow keys and closes on ESC

## Implementation Steps
1. Unplug mouse; walk through app
2. Catalog any focus escape, lost focus, or unreachable control
3. Fix at primitive level (Dialog.focusTrap, Menu.navigation)

## Files
- src/components/ui/*

## Notes
Base UI ships focus-trap and escape handling, but the brutalist wrappers must forward these props correctly.

title: Axe accessibility smoke test — zero new violations
description:
## Context
Part of QA & visual regression (gviz-rdr). Ensure the brutalist rebuild doesn't regress accessibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] axe-core run against the running app (via `@axe-core/react` in dev or cypress-axe)
- [ ] Zero new violations vs pre-migration baseline
- [ ] Focus traps present in every Dialog (focus stays in modal, restores on close)
- [ ] Every IconButton has an aria-label
- [ ] Every form <input> has an associated <label> (explicit `htmlFor` or wrapping)

## Implementation Steps
1. Install or enable axe (cypress-axe if not present)
2. Run sweep across all primary routes and opened dialogs
3. Triage each violation; fix at component or primitive level
4. Add a regression test that fails on new violations

## Files
- src/**/*.js

## Notes
Focus on P0/P1 axe rules: color-contrast, aria-*, label, region.

title: Manual QA matrix — walk every menu, dialog, drawer, form, button
description:

title: Run cypress integration suite and triage failures
description:
## Context
Part of QA & visual regression (gviz-rdr). Cypress integration tests likely break on MUI-specific selectors, aria-labels, and role queries after the brutalist migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cypress suite (`npm run integration-test` or equivalent) runs to completion
- [ ] All failures triaged and fixed
- [ ] Selectors updated away from MUI class names toward semantic queries
- [ ] aria-label and role coverage restored where missing

## Implementation Steps
1. Run integration test suite
2. Catalog each failure: selector break, aria-label, role, timing
3. Fix selectors to be resilient (prefer data-testid or aria)
4. Ensure brutalist components expose the same roles/labels as MUI predecessors

## Files
- cypress/** (or equivalent integration test dir)

## Notes
Most likely breaks: menus no longer have role=menu if primitive differs, IconButton aria-label missing, Dialog aria-modal attribute.

title: Run npm test and triage MUI-class-name failures
description:
## Context
Part of QA & visual regression (gviz-rdr). Existing tests may assert MUI CSS class names or MUI-specific DOM shapes that no longer apply post-migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npm test` runs to completion
- [ ] Any test asserting MUI class names (e.g. `.MuiButton-root`) rewritten to use semantic queries (getByRole, getByLabelText)
- [ ] Any test querying MUI-specific DOM structure updated to brutalist equivalents
- [ ] All tests pass on main post-migration

## Implementation Steps
1. Run `npm test` and capture failures
2. For each failure, identify root cause: MUI selector, MUI ref, aria mismatch
3. Rewrite using @testing-library semantic queries
4. Re-run until green

## Files
- src/**/*.test.js

## Notes
Focus on not weakening assertion strength — replace `.MuiButton-root` with `getByRole('button', {name})` not with `querySelector('button')`.

title: EPIC: Brutalist redesign — replace MUI with Base UI
description:
## Context
Migrate the entire human interface from Material-UI (@mui/material, @mui/icons-material, tss-react, typeface-roboto) to a custom brutalist/minimalist design built on Base UI primitives (@base-ui-components/react) with hand-picked typography. Every event handler, state shape, and prop wiring must remain byte-identical — pure visual + dependency change. This is the master tracking epic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No imports from @mui/* anywhere in src/
- [ ] No imports from tss-react anywhere in src/
- [ ] typeface-roboto removed
- [ ] All 22 component files migrated
- [ ] All 10 dialogs visually match new style guide
- [ ] Cypress integration suite still green
- [ ] Jest suite still green
- [ ] Bundle size reduced (record before/after)

## Implementation Steps
1. Land Foundation epic (tokens, fonts, primitive scaffolding)
2. Land Primitives epic (one bd per MUI primitive replacement)
3. Land Layout/Nav, Dialogs, Surfaces epics in parallel
4. QA pass
5. Cleanup epic — remove MUI deps

## Files
- src/components/ui/* (new)
- src/components/icons/* (new)
- src/design/* (new)
- All existing src/*.js consumers

## Notes
Style rules: 0 border-radius, 1-2px solid borders, monochrome + #4ed1f8 accent, Space Grotesk + JetBrains Mono, 4/8/16/24/32 spacing, linear 80ms transitions.

## Context

- `import { CircularProgress } from '@mui/material';`

- `import { CloseFullscreen as CloseFullscreenIcon } from '@mui/icons-material';`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zero `@mui` / `tss-react` imports remain
- [ ] Fade replaced with a simple CSS transition wrapper

- [ ] IconButton replaced with brutalist equivalent

831 lines — do not refactor logic while migrating. Preserve d3-graphviz refs, zoom behavior, and ref-forwarding. 30 min budget.

title: Add bd remember knowledge: brutalist style decisions
description:
## Context
Capture the brutalist design rationale + non-obvious tradeoffs (why no border-radius, why monochrome+single accent, why hard offset shadows) using `bd remember` so future sessions don't relitigate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least 3 `bd remember` entries covering: color palette rationale, typography choice, motion philosophy

## Implementation Steps
1. bd remember 'Brutalist redesign: monochrome + single #4ed1f8 accent because the editor surface is colorful (graphs); chrome must not compete.'
2. bd remember 'Typography: Space Grotesk for UI / JetBrains Mono for code-adjacent labels — the app is a code editor; mono labels reinforce the medium.'
3. bd remember 'Motion: linear 80ms transitions only; no easing curves. Brutalism rejects organic movement.'

## Files
(none — uses bd memory)
